(born November 19, 1989), known professionally as **Tyga** ([/ˈtaɪɡə/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); a [backronym](/wiki/Backronym "Backronym") for **Thank You God Always**),[[3]](#cite_note-3) is an American rapper, singer, and actor. After several independent releases, he signed a recording contract with [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne")'s [Young Money Entertainment](/wiki/Young_Money_Entertainment "Young Money Entertainment"), an imprint of [Cash Money Records](/wiki/Cash_Money_Records "Cash Money Records") and [Republic Records](/wiki/Republic_Records "Republic Records") in 2008. His second album and major label debut, *[Careless World: Rise of the Last King](/wiki/Careless_World:_Rise_of_the_Last_King "Careless World: Rise of the Last King")* (2012), peaked at number four on the [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200"), received [platinum](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification") certification by the [Recording Industry Association of America](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America") (RIAA), and was met with critical praise. It spawned the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100")-top ten single "[Rack City](/wiki/Rack_City "Rack City")", as well as the top 40 single "[Faded](/wiki/Faded_(Tyga_song) "Faded (Tyga song)")" (featuring [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne")). His third album, *[Hotel California](/wiki/Hotel_California_(Tyga_album) "Hotel California (Tyga album)")* (2013), was met with trailing critical and commercial response, along with his collaborative album with singer [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown "Chris Brown"), *[Fan of a Fan: The Album](/wiki/Fan_of_a_Fan:_The_Album "Fan of a Fan: The Album")* (2015).

**Micheal Ray Stevenson** (born November 19, 1989), known professionally as **Tyga** ([/ˈtaɪɡə/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); a [backronym](/wiki/Backronym "Backronym") for **Thank You God Always**),[[3]](#cite_note-3) is an American rapper, singer, and actor. After several independent releases, he signed a recording contract with [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne")'s [Young Money Entertainment](/wiki/Young_Money_Entertainment "Young Money Entertainment"), an imprint of [Cash Money Records](/wiki/Cash_Money_Records "Cash Money Records") and [Republic Records](/wiki/Republic_Records "Republic Records") in 2008. His second album and major label debut, *[Careless World: Rise of the Last King](/wiki/Careless_World:_Rise_of_the_Last_King "Careless World: Rise of the Last King")* (2012), peaked at number four on the [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200"), received [platinum](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification") certification by the [Recording Industry Association of America](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America") (RIAA), and was met with critical praise. It spawned the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100")-top ten single "[Rack City](/wiki/Rack_City "Rack City")", as well as the top 40 single "[Faded](/wiki/Faded_(Tyga_song) "Faded (Tyga song)")" (featuring [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne")). His third album, *[Hotel California](/wiki/Hotel_California_(Tyga_album) "Hotel California (Tyga album)")* (2013), was met with trailing critical and commercial response, along with his collaborative album with singer [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown "Chris Brown"), *[Fan of a Fan: The Album](/wiki/Fan_of_a_Fan:_The_Album "Fan of a Fan: The Album")* (2015).

Due to disagreements with Young Money, Tyga's fourth album, *[The Gold Album: 18th Dynasty](/wiki/The_Gold_Album:_18th_Dynasty "The Gold Album: 18th Dynasty")* (2015), was released independently. Executive-produced by his then-common law relative [Kanye West](/wiki/Kanye_West "Kanye West"), it became his lowest-selling album to that point with estimated sales of 5,000 [album equivalent units](/wiki/Billboard_200#Incorporation_of_streaming_data_and_track_sales "Billboard 200") in its first week.[[4]](#cite_note-hotnewhiphop_sales-4) His fifth and sixth albums, *[BitchImTheShit2](/wiki/BitchImTheShit2 "BitchImTheShit2")* (2017) and *[Kyoto](/wiki/Kyoto_(Tyga_album) "Kyoto (Tyga album)")* (2018), were both met with continued stagnation. His May 2018 single, "[Taste](/wiki/Taste_(Tyga_song) "Taste (Tyga song)")" (featuring [Offset](/wiki/Offset_(rapper) "Offset (rapper)")), marked a brief period of commercial resurgence; it became his first [top 40](/wiki/Top_40 "Top 40") single since 2015's "[Ayo](/wiki/Ayo_(song) "Ayo (song)")" (with Chris Brown)—peaking at number eight on the *Billboard* Hot 100—and received [diamond](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification") certification by the RIAA. It served as lead single and preceded the release of his seventh album, *[Legendary](/wiki/Legendary_(Tyga_album) "Legendary (Tyga album)")* (2019).[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Early life

Micheal Ray Stevenson was born on November 19, 1989, in [Compton, California](/wiki/Compton,_California "Compton, California"),[[6]](#cite_note-allmusic-6) and raised there until he "was about 11, 12", before moving to adjacent [Gardena](/wiki/Gardena,_California "Gardena, California").[[7]](#cite_note-7) He was born to Jamaican and Vietnamese parents.[[8]](#cite_note-8) His mother, Pasionaye Nicole Nguyen (September 11, 1971 – January 18, 2025), was born in Vietnam and had the maiden name [Nguyen](/wiki/Nguyen "Nguyen").[[9]](#cite_note-californiabirthindex-9) He grew up listening to [Fabolous](/wiki/Fabolous "Fabolous"), [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne"), [Cam'ron](/wiki/Cam%27ron "Cam'ron"), and [Eminem](/wiki/Eminem "Eminem"), among others.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

On December 3, 2012, debate arose concerning Tyga's upbringing. Tyga claimed to have been brought up in the low [socioeconomic](/wiki/Socioeconomic "Socioeconomic") area of Compton, but in leaked footage from the unaired [television show](/wiki/Television_show "Television show") *Bustas,* he claimed he grew up in a "well-to-do" home in [the Valley](/wiki/San_Fernando_Valley "San Fernando Valley"), where his parents drove a [Range Rover](/wiki/Range_Rover "Range Rover"). He also claimed that he attained his nickname from his mother calling him [Tiger Woods](/wiki/Tiger_Woods "Tiger Woods").[[11]](#cite_note-11) There has been speculation that his claims on the show were satirical and not to be taken seriously. Tyga responded to the video's leak on [Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter"): "When u 14 an ambitious u don't give a fuk about anything. Scripted tv isn't that what we all live for. Hahahaha."[[12]](#cite_note-12)

## Career

### 2007–2010: *No Introduction* and mixtapes

> I met Tyga, he handed me a mixtape on [Melrose](/wiki/Melrose_Avenue "Melrose Avenue"). I was out in L.A. shopping. (...) You know, being on the road, I get demos and mixtapes all the time. Me and Matt, the drummer of [Gym Class](/wiki/Gym_Class_Heroes "Gym Class Heroes"), we had this running joke like, “Let's start the record label called Roadside Records”. The joke was, “Yeah give us your demo and we’ll sign you to Roadside Records.” Our motto was “your demo hits the streets the same night” which means as soon as we get your demo, we throwing them bitches out the bus window. (laughs)
> For some reason, I didn’t throw Tyga’s out. I kept it and listened to it like, homie got potential and he had a lot of energy. So I took him under my wing.

—Travie McCoy on discovering Tyga in a 2021 interview with *[HotNewHipHop](/wiki/HotNewHipHop "HotNewHipHop")*.[[13]](#cite_note-McCoy-13)

Tyga's career began with him releasing a multitude of demo tapes and mixtapes. His 2007 debut mixtape, *Young On Probation*, garnered the attention of rapper and [Gym Class Heroes](/wiki/Gym_Class_Heroes "Gym Class Heroes") frontman, [Travie McCoy](/wiki/Travie_McCoy "Travie McCoy"). Seeing talent in the artist, McCoy led him to sign a recording contract with [Fall Out Boy](/wiki/Fall_Out_Boy "Fall Out Boy") bassist [Pete Wentz](/wiki/Pete_Wentz "Pete Wentz")'s independent label [Decaydence Records](/wiki/Decaydance_Records "Decaydance Records").[[14]](#cite_note-:0-14) Tyga ended up performing on Fall Out Boy's "[This Ain't a Scene, It's an Arms Race](/wiki/This_Ain%27t_a_Scene,_It%27s_an_Arms_Race#Remix "This Ain't a Scene, It's an Arms Race")" remix with McCoy and Lil' Wayne at the [2007 MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/2007_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2007 MTV Video Music Awards").[[14]](#cite_note-:0-14) He also performed at gigs with Gym Class Heroes and received mentoring from McCoy.[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[14]](#cite_note-:0-14)[[16]](#cite_note-16) McCoy stated that he "had [Tyga] sending me verses every other day. We started doing interviews, and he was like, 'This my big cousin Trav.' So a lot of people had the misunderstanding we were blood-related".[[13]](#cite_note-McCoy-13)

On June 10, 2008, Tyga released his debut studio album, *[No Introduction](/wiki/No_Introduction "No Introduction")* McCoy's imprint, BatSquad Records.[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-ProHipHop-18) It was preceded by the single "[Coconut Juice](/wiki/Coconut_Juice_(song) "Coconut Juice (song)")," featuring McCoy, which became his first entry on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100"). In addition to McCoy, the music video featured appearances by Pete Wentz and Lil' Wayne.[[14]](#cite_note-:0-14) The album peaked at number 112 on the [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") and received mixed reviews. A song from the album, "Diamond Life" was used in the video games *[Need for Speed: Undercover](/wiki/Need_for_Speed:_Undercover "Need for Speed: Undercover")* and *[Madden NFL 2009](/wiki/Madden_NFL_2009 "Madden NFL 2009")*, as well as in the 2009 film *[Fighting](/wiki/Fighting_(2009_film) "Fighting (2009 film)")*.

Around this time, Tyga formed the record label G.E.D. Inc. (Grinding every day, Getting every dollar, or Getting educated daily) around the time, the same imprint that helped launch the career of fellow [West Coast](/wiki/West_Coast_hip_hop "West Coast hip hop") rapper [Schoolboy Q](/wiki/Schoolboy_Q "Schoolboy Q"), with whom he has worked with early in his career. Tyga also has a *G.E.D. Inc* tattoo above his right eyebrow.[[19]](#cite_note-19)[[20]](#cite_note-20)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1c/Tyga.jpg/250px-Tyga.jpg)](/wiki/File:Tyga.jpg)

Tyga in 2008

In late 2008, Tyga signed a new record deal with [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne")'s [Young Money Entertainment](/wiki/Young_Money_Entertainment "Young Money Entertainment"), which was a subsidiary of [Cash Money Records](/wiki/Cash_Money_Records "Cash Money Records") at the time. He appeared on the label's [posse cut](/wiki/Posse_cut "Posse cut") hit single "[Bedrock](/wiki/Bedrock_(song) "Bedrock (song)")" shortly after his signing. He later released the mixtape *Fan of a Fan* with singer [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown "Chris Brown") and producers DJ Ill Will and DJ Rockstar. It featured other artists such as [Bow Wow](/wiki/Bow_Wow_(rapper) "Bow Wow (rapper)"), Lil Wayne, and [Kevin McCall](/wiki/Kevin_McCall "Kevin McCall") featured in several tracks. Four tracks — "Holla at Me", "G Shit", "[No Bullshit](/wiki/No_Bullshit "No Bullshit")" and "[Deuces](/wiki/Deuces_(song) "Deuces (song)")" — were accompanied with music videos. The track "Deuces" was released as the lead single for the mixtape and reached number 14 on the *Billboard* Hot 100 and number one on the *Billboard* [Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs](/wiki/Hot_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Songs "Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs"); it was also nominated for [Best Rap/Sung Collaboration](/wiki/Best_Rap/Sung_Collaboration "Best Rap/Sung Collaboration") at the [53rd Grammy Awards](/wiki/53rd_Grammy_Awards "53rd Grammy Awards").[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22) Tyga told MTV in an interview that the mixtape's production took just short of a week.[[23]](#cite_note-23) "Deuces" was eventually included on Brown's fourth studio album, *[F.A.M.E.](/wiki/F.A.M.E._(Chris_Brown_album) "F.A.M.E. (Chris Brown album)")* (2011).

### 2011–2012: *Careless World: Rise of the Last King*

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f5/Tyga_2012.png/250px-Tyga_2012.png)](/wiki/File:Tyga_2012.png)

Tyga in 2012

*Careless World: Rise of the Last King* was his second studio album. The album was released on February 21, 2012, on Young Money Entertainment, [Cash Money Records](/wiki/Cash_Money_Records "Cash Money Records"), and [Universal Republic Records](/wiki/Universal_Republic_Records "Universal Republic Records"). It was a shift from the [poppy dance](/wiki/Pop_rap "Pop rap") sound exemplified on his previous effort, *[No Introduction](/wiki/No_Introduction "No Introduction")*, and geared towards the [gangsta rap](/wiki/Gangsta_rap "Gangsta rap") style first heard in his collaborative album with Young Money, *[We Are Young Money](/wiki/We_Are_Young_Money "We Are Young Money")* (2009).[[24]](#cite_note-Martin-24) Tyga stated that the album shows he has grown up but still relates to the kids. In preparation for the album, Tyga released the mixtape *#BitchImTheShit* in December 2011.

The album was preceded by four singles. Its first, "[Far Away](/wiki/Far_Away_(Tyga_Song) "Far Away (Tyga Song)")" (featuring [Chris Richardson](/wiki/Chris_Richardson "Chris Richardson")), was released on May 17, 2011, and peaked at 86 on the *Billboard* Hot 100. Its second, "[Still Got It](/wiki/Still_Got_It "Still Got It")" (featuring labelmate [Drake](/wiki/Drake_(musician) "Drake (musician)")), was released on October 4, 2011, and peaked at 89 on the *Billboard* Hot 100. Its third single, "[Rack City](/wiki/Rack_City "Rack City")", was released on December 6, 2011. It first appeared on Tyga's *Well Done 2* mixtape, and was later listed to the album following its commercial success. It debuted at number 94 on the *Billboard* Hot 100, and peaked at number eight. Its fourth single, "[Faded](/wiki/Faded_(Tyga_song) "Faded (Tyga song)")" (featuring label boss [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne")), was released on January 13, 2012. It peaked at number 33 on the chart.

For the remainder of that year, Tyga made several guest appearances on other singles, most notably on the *Billboard* Hot 100 entries "Snitches Ain't…" by [YG](/wiki/YG_(rapper) "YG (rapper)"), "[Ayy Ladies](/wiki/Ayy_Ladies "Ayy Ladies")" by [Travis Porter](/wiki/Travis_Porter "Travis Porter"), and "[Get Low](/wiki/Get_Low_(Waka_Flocka_Flame_song) "Get Low (Waka Flocka Flame song)")" by [Waka Flocka Flame](/wiki/Waka_Flocka_Flame "Waka Flocka Flame"). In August, he released the mixtape *[Well Done 3](/wiki/Well_Done_3 "Well Done 3")*, which spawned by the single "[Do My Dance](/wiki/Do_My_Dance "Do My Dance")" (featuring [2 Chainz](/wiki/2_Chainz "2 Chainz")). The song peaked at number 79 on the *Billboard* Hot 100.

Also that year, Tyga also produced and co-directed (with Justice Young) a multi-[AVN Award](/wiki/AVN_Awards "AVN Awards")-nominated crossover [adult film](/wiki/Adult_film "Adult film"), *Rack City: The XXX Movie*, which starred [Skin Diamond](/wiki/Skin_Diamond "Skin Diamond"), Jada Fire, [Ice La Fox](https://ru.wikipedia.org/wiki/%D0%9B%D0%B0_%D0%A4%D0%BE%D0%BA%D1%81,_%D0%90%D0%B9%D1%81 "ru:Ла Фокс, Айс"), Sophie Dee, [Lexington Steele](/wiki/Lexington_Steele "Lexington Steele"), Kristina Rose, London Keyes and Daisy Marie.[[25]](#cite_note-biavn-25)[[26]](#cite_note-26) He also signed the soundtrack, which includes the hits "Rack City" and "Faded" together with several original songs, and he appeared in the film in a non-sex role.[[27]](#cite_note-EXCLUSIVE:_Hip-Hop_Star_Tyga_Directs,_Stars_In_'Rack_City_XXX'-27)[[28]](#cite_note-Tyga_Directs_Adult_Film_'Rack_City:_The_XXX_Movie'-28)[[29]](#cite_note-Tyga_Making_His_Directorial_Debut-29)[[30]](#cite_note-Tyga_Lands_Three_AVN_Award_Nominations-30)[[31]](#cite_note-AVN_Nods_To_Tyga_with_Three_Award_Nominations-31)[[32]](#cite_note-Tyga_Scores_3_AVN_Award_Nominations-32)

### 2012–2013: *Hotel California*

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fa/Tyga_2013.jpg/250px-Tyga_2013.jpg)](/wiki/File:Tyga_2013.jpg)

Tyga in 2013

Tyga's third studio album, *[Hotel California](/wiki/Hotel_California_(Tyga_album) "Hotel California (Tyga album)")*, was released on April 9, 2013. The first single, "[Dope](/wiki/Dope_(Tyga_song) "Dope (Tyga song)")", was released on December 25. The sported a feature from rapper [Rick Ross](/wiki/Rick_Ross "Rick Ross").[[33]](#cite_note-www.hiphopdx.com-33) Before the single dropped Tyga released a nine-track mixtape under the name 187.[[34]](#cite_note-34) *187* the mixtape was released on November 30, 2012. It features remixes to songs such as [2 Chainz](/wiki/2_Chainz "2 Chainz")'s "[I'm Different](/wiki/I%27m_Different_(2_Chainz_song) "I'm Different (2 Chainz song)")", [Meek Mill](/wiki/Meek_Mill "Meek Mill")'s "[Young & Gettin' It](/wiki/Young_%26_Gettin%27_It "Young & Gettin' It")" and [GOOD Music](/wiki/GOOD_Music "GOOD Music")'s "[Clique](/wiki/Clique_(song) "Clique (song)")", among others and new material.[[35]](#cite_note-187_mixtape_release-35)[[33]](#cite_note-www.hiphopdx.com-33) On episode three of Tyga's tour vlog Tyga confirmed four features on the upcoming album, including [Rick Ross](/wiki/Rick_Ross "Rick Ross"), [Nicki Minaj](/wiki/Nicki_Minaj "Nicki Minaj"), [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown "Chris Brown"), and [Wiz Khalifa](/wiki/Wiz_Khalifa "Wiz Khalifa").[[36]](#cite_note-36) Tyga performed at Harvard University's Yardfest in the Spring of 2013, despite opposition from critics who've signed a petition seeking to keep him away over what they call his "violently [misogynistic](/wiki/Misogynistic "Misogynistic") lyrics".[[37]](#cite_note-37) At this time, Tyga collaborated with singer [Sabi](/wiki/Sabi_(singer) "Sabi (singer)") on her song "Cali Love".[[38]](#cite_note-38)

*Hotel California* was released on April 9, 2013. The album featured guest appearances from Lil Wayne, Chris Brown, Rick Ross, 2 Chainz, [Game](/wiki/Game_(rapper) "Game (rapper)"), Wiz Khalifa, [Future](/wiki/Future_(rapper) "Future (rapper)") and [Jadakiss](/wiki/Jadakiss "Jadakiss"). Along with [production](/wiki/Record_production "Record production") by [Cool & Dre](/wiki/Cool_%26_Dre "Cool & Dre"), [D. A. Doman](/wiki/D._A._Doman "D. A. Doman"), [DJ Mustard](/wiki/DJ_Mustard "DJ Mustard"), [Detail](/wiki/Detail_(musician) "Detail (musician)"), Mars of [1500 or Nothin'](/wiki/1500_or_Nothin%27 "1500 or Nothin'"), [The Olympicks](/wiki/The_Olympicks "The Olympicks"), and [SAP](/wiki/SAP_(producer) "SAP (producer)") among others. The album was supported by three official singles — "[Dope](/wiki/Dope_(Tyga_song) "Dope (Tyga song)")" which features Rick Ross, "[For the Road](/wiki/For_the_Road "For the Road")" featuring Chris Brown, and "[Show You](/wiki/Show_You "Show You")" featuring Future, along with the promotional single "[Molly](/wiki/Molly_(Tyga_song) "Molly (Tyga song)")". The album was met with generally mixed reviews from [music critics](/wiki/Music_criticism "Music criticism").[[39]](#cite_note-39) It also was less successful than his debut album commercially, debuting at number seven on the U.S. [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") with first-week sales of 54,000 copies in the United States.[[40]](#cite_note-40)

### 2013–2015: *The Gold Album: 18th Dynasty*

Main article: [The Gold Album: 18th Dynasty](/wiki/The_Gold_Album:_18th_Dynasty "The Gold Album: 18th Dynasty")

On August 27, 2013, Tyga released a new single with DJ Mustard, "Throw It Up". It was also revealed that he was working on two new mixtapes, *Black Thought 3* and *Well Done 4*, as well as *The Gold Album*.[[41]](#cite_note-41) On October 21, 2013, Tyga premiered "[Wait for a Minute](/wiki/Wait_for_a_Minute "Wait for a Minute")" featuring [Justin Bieber](/wiki/Justin_Bieber "Justin Bieber"). It was released to [iTunes](/wiki/ITunes_Music_Store "ITunes Music Store") the following day.[[42]](#cite_note-42) He then confirmed that an album would be released in early 2014. He also explained that the album would, be more "way less commercial". He also said "I felt like *Hotel California* was more commercial and I made more radio-type records. This album is straight rap."[[43]](#cite_note-43)
Then on April 9, 2014, Tyga released a song called "[Hookah](/wiki/Hookah_(Tyga_song) "Hookah (Tyga song)")" which features the rapper [Young Thug](/wiki/Young_Thug "Young Thug").[[44]](#cite_note-44)

On May 1, 2014, Tyga made a tweet referring to [Kanye West](/wiki/Kanye_West "Kanye West"), [Mike Dean](/wiki/Mike_Dean_(record_producer) "Mike Dean (record producer)"), and his own producer Jess Jackson with a hash-tag The Gold Album. Kanye West served as the executive producer for the album, which was slated for release on November 18, 2014.[[45]](#cite_note-45)[[46]](#cite_note-46) Tyga was also featured on [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown "Chris Brown")'s single "[Loyal](/wiki/Loyal_(Chris_Brown_song) "Loyal (Chris Brown song)")", he was featured on the video version, the song since then peaked at number nine on the US Billboard Hot 100.[[47]](#cite_note-47) On May 28, 2014, Tyga released a new song entitled "*Real Deal*" via [SoundCloud](/wiki/SoundCloud "SoundCloud").[[48]](#cite_note-48) "Real Deal" was later released on [iTunes](/wiki/ITunes "ITunes") on August 1, 2014.[[49]](#cite_note-49) On September 12, 2014, Tyga announced via his [instagram](/wiki/Instagram "Instagram") that his new single titled "[40 Mill](/wiki/40_Mill_(song) "40 Mill (song)")" would be produced by [Kanye West](/wiki/Kanye_West "Kanye West"), [Mike Dean](/wiki/Mike_Dean_(record_producer) "Mike Dean (record producer)"), Dupri and his own producer [Jess Jackson](/wiki/Jess_Jackson_(record_producer) "Jess Jackson (record producer)"), artwork, The release date was not revealed though.[[50]](#cite_note-50) The album was set to have features from [Justin Bieber](/wiki/Justin_Bieber "Justin Bieber"), [Young Thug](/wiki/Young_Thug "Young Thug"), [Kanye West](/wiki/Kanye_West "Kanye West"), [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown "Chris Brown"), and [Drake](/wiki/Drake_(rapper) "Drake (rapper)"). Tyga criticized Drake in an October 2014 interview, calling Drake "fake" and said that he did not like [Nicki Minaj](/wiki/Nicki_Minaj "Nicki Minaj").[[51]](#cite_note-51)

On October 21, 2014, Tyga lashed out on his record label Young Money Entertainment, reporting that his album was completed, but that his music was being "held hostage" by the label, who would not allow him to release it, and stated that he was considering leaking the album.[[52]](#cite_note-52) On December 4, 2014, Tyga made a tweet declaring his project, *[The Gold Album: 18th Dynasty](/wiki/The_Gold_Album:_18th_Dynasty "The Gold Album: 18th Dynasty")* would be released independently due to not being on good terms with label [Cash Money Records](/wiki/Cash_Money_Records "Cash Money Records").[[53]](#cite_note-53)
In February 2015, Tyga released a collaborative album with Chris Brown, titled *[Fan of a Fan: The Album](/wiki/Fan_of_a_Fan:_The_Album "Fan of a Fan: The Album")*. Supported by the No. 21 peaking *Billboard* single "[Ayo](/wiki/Ayo_(Chris_Brown_and_Tyga_song) "Ayo (Chris Brown and Tyga song)")", the album peaked at No. 7 on the *Billboard* Hot 200 and featured guest appearances from [50 Cent](/wiki/50_Cent "50 Cent"), [T.I.](/wiki/T.I. "T.I."), [Pusha T](/wiki/Pusha_T "Pusha T"), [Wale](/wiki/Wale_(rapper) "Wale (rapper)"), [Ty Dolla Sign](/wiki/Ty_Dolla_Sign "Ty Dolla Sign"), [Schoolboy Q](/wiki/Schoolboy_Q "Schoolboy Q"), [Boosie Badazz](/wiki/Boosie_Badazz "Boosie Badazz"), and [Fat Trel](/wiki/Fat_Trel "Fat Trel"). On June 23, 2015, Tyga released *The Gold Album* for streaming exclusively on [Spotify](/wiki/Spotify "Spotify") then later on [Apple Music](/wiki/Apple_Music "Apple Music"). The album became his worst selling album to date, selling roughly 2,000 units on its opening week.[[54]](#cite_note-54)

His reality show *[Kingin' with Tyga](/wiki/Kingin%27_with_Tyga "Kingin' with Tyga")* premiered on July 24, 2015, on [MTV2](/wiki/MTV2 "MTV2").[[55]](#cite_note-55)

### 2016–2017: Signing to GOOD Music and *BitchImTheShit2*

Main article: [BitchImTheShit2](/wiki/BitchImTheShit2 "BitchImTheShit2")

On September 7, 2016, Kanye West, who, like Tyga, was in a relationship to a [Kardashian](/wiki/Kardashian_family "Kardashian family") at the time, announced that he had signed Tyga to his [GOOD Music](/wiki/GOOD_Music "GOOD Music") imprint, under the aegis of [Def Jam Recordings](/wiki/Def_Jam_Recordings "Def Jam Recordings").[[56]](#cite_note-56)

On July 17, 2017, it was announced that Tyga will join as a series regular in the third season of the [VH1](/wiki/VH1 "VH1") slasher television series *[Scream](/wiki/Scream_(TV_series) "Scream (TV series)")*. He starred in the role of Jamal Elliot.[[57]](#cite_note-57) The season premiered on July 8, 2019.[[58]](#cite_note-58)

On July 21, 2017, Tyga released his fifth studio album, *[BitchImTheShit2](/wiki/BitchImTheShit2 "BitchImTheShit2")*, the sequel to his 2011 mixtape *#BitchImTheShit*.[[59]](#cite_note-59)

### 2018–present: *Kyoto*, commercial resurgence, *Legendary* and *NSFW*

Main articles: [Kyoto (Tyga album)](/wiki/Kyoto_(Tyga_album) "Kyoto (Tyga album)") and [Legendary (Tyga album)](/wiki/Legendary_(Tyga_album) "Legendary (Tyga album)")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/17/Tyga_at_VMAs.png/250px-Tyga_at_VMAs.png)](/wiki/File:Tyga_at_VMAs.png)

Tyga in 2018

On February 16, 2018, Tyga released his sixth studio album, *[Kyoto](/wiki/Kyoto_(Tyga_album) "Kyoto (Tyga album)")*. The album was panned by critics and sold poorly, becoming his first album to fail to chart anywhere.[[60]](#cite_note-60)[[61]](#cite_note-61)

On May 16, 2018, Tyga released the single "[Taste](/wiki/Taste_(Tyga_song) "Taste (Tyga song)")" featuring [Offset](/wiki/Offset_(rapper) "Offset (rapper)") of [Migos](/wiki/Migos "Migos"). The song was a hit, becoming one of the biggest songs of summer 2018 and peaking at number 8 on the US [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100"). It marked Tyga's first entry on the Hot 100 in three years, since 2015's "[Ride Out](/wiki/Ride_Out "Ride Out")", and is certified 9× [platinum](/wiki/Platinum_certification "Platinum certification") by the [Recording Industry Association of America](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America") (RIAA).[[62]](#cite_note-riaa.com-62) The song is considered the catalyst for Tyga's return to the mainstream music spotlight after almost 5 years of poor sales and negative reviews, being considered his "comeback" record.[[63]](#cite_note-63)[[64]](#cite_note-64)[[65]](#cite_note-65)

Following the success of "Taste", Tyga released a number of other singles throughout the latter of 2018, beginning with "Swish", which was certified [gold](/wiki/Gold_certification "Gold certification"),[[62]](#cite_note-riaa.com-62) and later released "[Dip](/wiki/Dip_(song) "Dip (song)")" featuring [Nicki Minaj](/wiki/Nicki_Minaj "Nicki Minaj"), which was certified platinum.[[62]](#cite_note-riaa.com-62) He was also featured on [Iggy Azalea](/wiki/Iggy_Azalea "Iggy Azalea")'s platinum single, "[Kream](/wiki/Kream_(song) "Kream (song)")" in 2018, and "[Sip It](/wiki/Sip_It "Sip It")" in 2021.[[62]](#cite_note-riaa.com-62) Tyga's seventh album *[Legendary](/wiki/Legendary_(Tyga_album) "Legendary (Tyga album)")* was released in June 2019.[[66]](#cite_note-66)

In 2019, YG released the single "[Go Loko](/wiki/Go_Loko "Go Loko")", which featured Tyga. He stated, "We wanted to do something different to kinda try to bring all Latins together" and "Even YG could tell you, he grew up around all Mexicans, so we really wanted to do something to give back to the culture."[[67]](#cite_note-67)

In July 2020, Tyga partnered with businessman [Robert Earl](/wiki/Robert_Earl "Robert Earl") to launch a virtual kitchen brand called Tyga Bites, which uses existing restaurants' kitchens to produce branded menu items such as boneless chicken wings for delivery service only.[[68]](#cite_note-68)

In 2021, Tyga competed on the [sixth season](/wiki/The_Masked_Singer_(American_TV_series)_season_6 "The Masked Singer (American TV series) season 6") of *[The Masked Singer](/wiki/The_Masked_Singer_(American_TV_series) "The Masked Singer (American TV series)")* as "Dalmatian". He was the first of Group B to be eliminated.[[69]](#cite_note-69)

In 2024, Tyga announced his eighth studio album, titled *NSFW* (short for "Not Safe for Work"). It was released on February 7, 2025.[[70]](#cite_note-70)

In May 2025, Tyga arrived in [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia") and performed at the [CSKA Arena](/wiki/CSKA_Arena "CSKA Arena") in Moscow.[[71]](#cite_note-71)

In 2025, Tyga featured on the [Ciara](/wiki/Ciara "Ciara") single "Dance with Me", appearing on the latter's eighth studio album, *[CiCi](/wiki/CiCi_(album) "CiCi (album)")*, which was released on August 22, 2025.[[72]](#cite_note-72)[[73]](#cite_note-73)

## Personal life

On September 6, 2010, Tyga married Jordan Craig.[[1]](#cite_note-Enews-1) He filed for divorce one month later, and it was finalized in 2011.[[74]](#cite_note-74)

Tyga met model [Blac Chyna](/wiki/Blac_Chyna "Blac Chyna") on October 5, 2011, at The F.A.M.E Tour After Party with Chris Brown at King of Diamonds in [Miami](/wiki/Miami "Miami").[[75]](#cite_note-75)[[76]](#cite_note-76) Blac Chyna was cast as Tyga's leading lady in his "[Rack City](/wiki/Rack_City "Rack City")" music video.[[77]](#cite_note-autogenerated1-77) They became engaged on November 9, 2011, and posed for the cover of *Urban Ink* magazine in March 2012.[[78]](#cite_note-78) On October 16, 2012, their son, named King Cairo,[[79]](#cite_note-79) was born,[[80]](#cite_note-80) and Tyga purchased a $6.5 million mansion in [Calabasas, California](/wiki/Calabasas,_California "Calabasas, California") that same month.[[81]](#cite_note-81) Both parents have tattoos of their son's name.[[82]](#cite_note-82)[[83]](#cite_note-83) The couple split in 2014.[[84]](#cite_note-84)

After months of speculation, Tyga confirmed his relationship with reality star [Kylie Jenner](/wiki/Kylie_Jenner "Kylie Jenner") in August 2015. However, this relationship sparked controversy due to the age disparity, and the fact that they were reportedly dating since Jenner was 16. The legal age of consent in the state of California is 18.[[85]](#cite_note-85)[[86]](#cite_note-86) Photos of him and Jenner first seen holding hands in [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") went viral a few days after her 18th birthday.[[87]](#cite_note-87) The couple split in April 2017.[[88]](#cite_note-88)

Tyga dated British social model [Demi Rose](/wiki/Demi_Rose_Mawby "Demi Rose Mawby") for a short time in 2016.[[89]](#cite_note-89)

On February 21, 2025, Tyga announced that his mother, Pasionaye Nicole Nguyen, had died at age 53 a month earlier on January 18.[[90]](#cite_note-90)

### Robbery

In June 2008, Tyga was robbed of his jewelry by unknown assailants. A pair of diamond chains would end up in the hands of local rapper [40 Glocc](/wiki/40_Glocc "40 Glocc"), who showed them off in an online video.[[91]](#cite_note-91) Tyga would eventually admit to the robbery, but said 40 Glocc wasn't behind it. "Some niggas seen me and ran up on me with a little burner... I guess 40 Glocc's peoples robbed the peoples that got me, and then hopped on [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube"), talking about they robbed me."[[92]](#cite_note-92)

### Legal issues

In late 2012, Tyga was sued by two women who appeared in the "[Make It Nasty](/wiki/Make_It_Nasty "Make It Nasty")" video for $10 million each; they claimed he had shown their nipples without their consent. They were assured that this would be edited out and the unedited version would not be released, but Tyga proceeded to release a fully unedited version.[[93]](#cite_note-93) On September 21, 2013, another woman from the video sued Tyga for [sexual battery](/wiki/Sexual_battery "Sexual battery"), fraud, invasion of privacy, and infliction of emotional distress. She claims, like the two other women who have filed suit against him, that she was convinced to dance topless and assured her breasts would be edited out.[[94]](#cite_note-94)

On September 4, 2013, it was reported Tyga had been sued by [Beverly Hills](/wiki/Beverly_Hills "Beverly Hills")-based jeweler Jason Arasheben for allegedly not paying for roughly $91,000 worth of jewelry. Arasheben claims that Tyga agreed to pay $28,275 for the jeweler's diamond pantheon watch in August 2012 and that he borrowed a $63,000 diamond [Cuban link chain](/wiki/Cuban_link_chain "Cuban link chain") in December 2012 and never returned the item, so he sued Tyga for the cost of both pieces, plus late fees, which totaled up to $185,306.50 in damages, double the original total cost.[[95]](#cite_note-95) Tyga fell ill before the interrogation of this debt and was considered not able to proceed. He also did not bring the documents he was supposed to, however, he paid $100,000 of the $200,000 owed to his client.[[96]](#cite_note-96)

In June 2015, a judge ordered Tyga to pay an $80,000 settlement to a former landlord, who rented a home to the rapper in the city of Calabasas, California.[[97]](#cite_note-97) It was the second ruling in the case, following a judgment in May 2015, in which he was ordered to pay $90,000, though the debt was never paid.[[98]](#cite_note-98) In September 2015, the state of California placed a [tax lien](/wiki/Tax_lien "Tax lien") on Tyga for $19,000 in unpaid taxes.[[99]](#cite_note-99) On his birthday, he was given a lawsuit in 2016.[[100]](#cite_note-100)

In October 2021, Tyga was arrested by police over domestic abuse charges.[[101]](#cite_note-101)
